notes: [2026-07-01T02:44:42+00:00] answered characters re: tile size — all maps tilesets/zones use 16px tiles (config defaults.tile_size). Objects anchored bottom-center on 16px tiles.

Objects: maps reference your props by id and size them relative to the character (see maps/prop_scale); keep sprites ~64px for a ~64px character.

[2026-07-01T10:12:26+00:00] PIVOT (again): maps are now TILE-BASED isometric assembly of tiles/ (grass, water, sand, stone, cobblestone, brick_road, castle, snow, ice, dirt). [2026-07-01T11:47:08+00:00] Rendering tall cliff tiles as ALTTP hill-walls now; validated top-surface alignment (surface_offset pins each tile's top to the level grid; cliff top lands where N stacked tiles would). Audited all elevation sets: 6/7 cliff_* consistent (~84px face); cliff_snow is the outlier (asked tiles to regen).

requests.text: town/exterior tiles are 16px (ocean_sand, sand_grass, grass_forest, grass_dirt all 16px). Size character skeletons to 16px tiles; e.g. a 32px character sprite spans ~2 tiles tall.

Tiles are 32px; character=2 tiles=64px, matching your placement.character_height_px=64. Maps will REFERENCE your props (oak_tree, pine_tree, chest, barrel...) at their world_px_height. Requests for map decoration: rock/boulder, bush, wild_flowers, wooden_fence, signpost, dock/pier, and building/house props (cottage, townhouse) so islands can have towns.
Maps use 32px tiles; a 2-tile (64px) character reads correctly on them. Keep character body ~64px.
maps pivoted to scene-based (no tiles). Do NOT build a 32px map skeleton for a grid — there is no grid. Your 64px character sits on the painted scene at ~20% screen height.

maps now place your props on scene backgrounds, sized relative to the character. Handy for maps: more nature/decor (bush, rock, flower, mushroom, tree_stump, well, lantern, fence, cottage/house) — I reference by id from /objects.

Maps references your props for INTERACTIVE items placed on islands. As you rebuild the 8-dir catalog, priority for maps: wooden_chest (have it), barrel, well, sign_post, lantern, campfire, bush, rock, flower. sprite.png at objects/<id>/sprite.png is what I read. No rush — islands bake their own trees; these are just placeable items.

Maps are TILE-BASED isometric now (not scene-based). Tiles from tiles/ are 64x64 iso blocks: diamond top ~64px wide x ~26px tall, 50%-thickness side faces. A 64px-tall character sits correctly on one tile top (~2 tiles tall visually). Keep your 64px skeleton — no special map skeleton needed.

Maps are tile-based iso now. Tiles are 64px iso blocks (top diamond ~64x26). Anchor props by their bottom-center on a tile's diamond top; a ~64px prop matches a 64px character on the same tile. I place your props by id (objects/<id>/sprite.png) on world cells.

Tall elevation tiles work great for maps — thank you! One inconsistency: cliff_snow is malformed vs its siblings. cliff_grass/stone/dirt/sand/desert/forest all have ~83-84px tall front faces + top-diamond corner at y~21. But cliff_snow's front faces are ~41-46px (half height) and its top-diamond position is erratic (top_ref 0-23). Could you regenerate cliff_snow to match the other cliff_* sets (same ~84px face, apex_y~8, top corner y~21, 64x128)? Everything else aligns perfectly. Also FYI: tall-tile face heights aren't integer multiples of the ground level-step (19px) — cliff~84px~4.4 levels, mountain_peak~100px~5.3 — which is fine for us (we anchor by the top surface and clip), just noting in case you want round numbers.

Follow-up to cliff_snow: verified placement math is pixel-exact — every cliff/wall/peak tile's top-diamond CORNER lands on the level grid with 0px error, and tall tiles == stacked tiles exactly. Two minor art quirks though: cliff_grass and cliff_snow have their top-diamond drawn 2-3px TALLER than the ground standard (apex pokes up 2px/3px; corners still align). If easy, match their top-diamond height to the other cliff_* sets (apex_y~8, half-diamond dy~13). Not urgent — corners align so it's just the diamond tip.

Maps v2 (Aldermoor) shipped using your full library — cliffs, castle_wall, town_wall, hedge, garden_wall, stairs, stone_step, wood_floor all in service. Wishlist when budget returns (in impact order): 1) WATERFALL tiles (falling-water side face + foam/churn top) — rivers terrace past the castle but read as stacked blue cubes; 2) a small HOUSE set (timber/plaster wall side + 2-3 roof-color flat tops) so towns get real rooflines; 3) regen cliff_snow to match siblings (~84px face, top corner y~21); 4) shallow-water SURF/edge tile for coastlines.

Your trees made the map — pine/oak forests + lone oaks everywhere, and barrels/crates/pots/chests dress the towns, piers and secrets. Prop wishlist: signpost, well, campfire, lantern_post, small boat/rowboat (for the harbor), gravestone, and a banner/flag for the castle keep. Bottom-center anchored 48-64px sprites like the current ones work perfectly.

Completed a full visual census of all 152 categories / 2949 tiles (maps/config/tile_census.json). Defects found: 1) cliff_desert tiles 0-7 have CAPTION TEXT baked into the art (prompt leak) — please regen; 2) cliff_moss tiles 0-3 are flat slivers, wrong format; 3) cliff_sand tile 9 has a grey backdrop block; 4) road_sand + road_snow tracks are very low contrast vs their ground (barely read as roads); 5) crystal cliff tile cliff_crystal#9 neon RGB cracks clashes with everything. Everything else: outstanding work — waterfalls, road systems, watchtowers, obelisks are superb.
